description.txt: A workspace Owner using Soleur as their AI organization. Workspaces may have MULTIPLE Owners (ADR-038, ADR-073 team workspaces); Owner-shared surfaces (e.g. the operator email-triage inbox, ADR-066) are readable by every Owner, not just one founder.
Vendors, regulators, and counsel emailing the operator address. Their statutory/operational mail is the email-triage inbox's inbound source.

An untrusted contributor opening a pull request. Their PR-head code is executed only by the fix-constraints Stage A producer (pull_request, contents:read, no write token) — never by the privileged Stage B consumer, which opens a data-only draft follow-up PR from the default branch (ADR-074).
Claude LLM for agent reasoning and tool use
Source control, CI/CD, issue tracking, and releases
DNS, CDN, R2 storage, and zero-trust tunnel
Centralized secrets management with runtime injection
Community notifications and release announcements
Payment processing and subscription billing
Privacy-focused website analytics
Inbound email ingress (operator-inbox webhook, svix-verified) + transactional outbound (statutory triage notifications). ADR-066.

PRIVATE GHCR registry (ghcr.io/jikig-ai/soleur-web-platform + soleur-inngest-bootstrap) — source of the web app image a Hetzner host pulls (authenticated via a scoped read:packages credential; the host docker login is done by ci-deploy.sh / the fresh-boot bootstrap, #6005). The image also bakes the host bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl, extracted via docker cp at first boot to keep cloud-init user_data under Hetzner's 32,768-byte cap (ADR-080, #5921).

External uptime/heartbeat monitoring (BetterStackHQ/better-uptime). Apex + inngest/git-data heartbeats, and a per-host origin absence detector that probes each web host's CF-proxied hostname (web-N.app.soleur.ai/health) directly, so a dead/never-booted host pages with per-host attribution the round-robin monitors cannot give (#5933 Item 1).

Keyless container-image signing/verification (cosign/Fulcio/Rekor). The release CI keyless-signs the pushed web app image digest (this IS a live sigstore call — github → sigstore below). The host then cosign-verifies the signature FULLY OFFLINE against a pinned trusted_root.json — it makes NO live sigstore call at verify time (ADR-086, #6005) — supply-chain authenticity for the shared host (#5933 Item 4).
Next.js PWA providing dashboard and conversation UI
Claude Code instances executing agent workflows
plugins/soleur/ — skills, agents, and knowledge base
Hosting, data, and zero-trust access
Conversation UI, knowledge base viewer, session management

REST endpoints for auth, sessions, and agent control. Registers in-process Agent-SDK options.hooks (sandbox, subagent-audit, phase-surface, tool-attempt-telemetry) on web agent sessions — settingSources:[] isolates these from the CLI .claude/ shell Hook Engine (ADR-070 #5772, #5843).
JWT authentication, OAuth providers, session tokens
Executes agent workflows with full orchestration tools
Discovers and loads skills, agents, commands from plugin directory
Enforces syntactic rules — blocks commits to main, rm -rf, etc.
61 workflow skills — brainstorm, plan, work, review, compound, etc.
65 domain agents across 8 departments
Conventions, learnings, ADRs, specs, plans, brainstorms
Classifies intent and routes to workflow skills
Populates knowledge-base from existing codebase
Lists all available commands, skills, and agents
Explores requirements with domain leader assessment
Creates implementation plans with research and domain review
Executes plans with incremental commits and test-first
Multi-agent code review with 8 parallel reviewers
Captures learnings and promotes to constitution
Validates artifacts, creates PR, manages merge lifecycle
Full autonomous pipeline: plan, work, review, compound, ship
ADR lifecycle and C4 diagram generation
Engineering assessment, architecture decision detection
Marketing assessment, content opportunities
Product strategy, UX flow analysis
Architectural compliance and ADR coverage check
Validation-gates classifier-skill block edits (no-regression + target-passes)
Generates the L1 dep-cruiser import-boundary gate (client→server-secret) plus the two-stage pull_request→workflow_run auto-recovery workflows (ADR-074) into the product codebase CI
Users, BYOK-encrypted API keys, conversation sessions

Severity-ranked operational-notification store (ADR-085, #6007). Mutable read/act/archive state, workspace-grain, Owner-shared reads — SEPARATE from the email-triage WORM statutory ledger. Content-minimized (title + source_ref ids only). GET /api/inbox merges it with email-triage into one severity-ordered feed (statutory pinned first).
Zero-trust inbound access — no exposed ports

Cluster of Hetzner Cloud hosts (Nomad clients, spread placement) running web app + CLI engine containers; per-user worktrees on host-local NVMe (ADR-068). At first boot a fresh host pulls the web app image from GHCR and extracts its baked bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl via docker cp (ADR-080, #5921)
Durable trigger layer for server-side agents — cron, oneshot, and HTTP-armed reminders (ADR-030)

platform.infra.inngestPostgres.technology: PostgreSQL — dedicated EU project, Supavisor session pooler :5432

description.txt: Inngest config + run history. Dedicated project isolates connection budget + fate from the main app DB (#5450). Public tables RLS-locked; reachable only as the postgres owner (ADR-030 I8)

platform.infra.inngestPostgres.title: Supabase PostgreSQL (Inngest)

description.txt: Inngest durable queue + run-state. AOF on the persistent volume survives a host re-provision — the armed-reminder durability fix (#5450)

Co-located stateless sticky router (in each web-host process, not a separate box) — resolves a session's owning host from the per-user worktree lease at the WS-upgrade handshake; local ⇒ serve, remote ⇒ proxy over one-way-TLS private net. Control ops are host-local (no cross-host forwarding). Lease in Postgres (ADR-068 user-sticky amendment)
Placement, health-reschedule, and rolling deploys across the Hetzner cluster (ADR-068 Phase 4a)

platform.infra.gitDataStore.technology: Bare git repos over private net

description.txt: Shared bare git repos (objects/refs) over the private net; per-user worktrees stay host-local on NVMe. The git-data host holds the per-ref monotonic max generation and writer-side-CAS-rejects stale lease writes (ADR-068)

platform.infra.gitDataStore.title: Shared git-data

description.txt: Self-hosted EU Redis — ADR-059 replay buffer + session/control state; network-reachable, per-workspace key namespacing, TLS + requirepass. DISTINCT node from the loopback Inngest Redis (ADR-068 Phase 4a)

1gp96mb.title: Reads release notes for the in-app Releases page

1ybekua.title: Sends email to the operator address

9cdpdt.title: notifyInboxItem writes inbox_item + GET /api/inbox reads the merged feed (RLS Owner-scoped)

b2va0t.title: inbox_list agent tool reads the same merged severity-ranked feed (agent-native parity)

n13kz6.title: Opens untrusted PR — head code runs in fix-constraints Stage A (pull_request, contents:read, no write token) (ADR-074)

1q735fq.title: fix-constraints Stage B (workflow_run, PRIVILEGED, contents:write): opens a data-only draft follow-up PR via Git Data API — never checks out the untrusted tree (ADR-074)

1oi5nay.title: Statutory triage notifications (single configured recipient; reads are Owner-shared per ADR-066)

158u16.title: Pulls app image + baked bootstrap scripts/hooks at boot; authenticated (read:packages) since the packages are private; a web-2-recreate boot pins an immutable @sha256 digest (coherence-checked vs host_scripts_content_hash, not :latest); the cosign signature bundle is fetched here too — verify is offline against a pinned root, so there is no live sigstore call (#5921, #5887, #6005)

j4ognd.title: Per-host origin uptime probe (web-N.app.soleur.ai/health, CF-proxied) — absence detector (#5933 Item 1)

1n8i7fq.title: Keyless-signs the released app image digest (cosign, OIDC→Fulcio→Rekor) (#5933 Item 4)

External uptime/heartbeat monitoring (BetterStackHQ/better-uptime). Apex + inngest/git-data heartbeats, and a per-host origin absence detector that probes each web host's CF-proxied hostname (web-N.app.soleur.ai/health) directly, so a dead/never-booted host pages with per-host attribution the round-robin monitors cannot give (#5933 Item 1).
Next.js PWA providing dashboard and conversation UI
Claude Code instances executing agent workflows
Hosting, data, and zero-trust access

Severity-ranked operational-notification store (ADR-085, #6007). Mutable read/act/archive state, workspace-grain, Owner-shared reads — SEPARATE from the email-triage WORM statutory ledger. Content-minimized (title + source_ref ids only). GET /api/inbox merges it with email-triage into one severity-ordered feed (statutory pinned first).

Self-hosted EU Redis — ADR-059 replay buffer + session/control state; network-reachable, per-workspace key namespacing, TLS + requirepass. DISTINCT node from the loopback Inngest Redis (ADR-068 Phase 4a)

nodes.title: Shared git-data

description.txt: Shared bare git repos (objects/refs) over the private net; per-user worktrees stay host-local on NVMe. The git-data host holds the per-ref monotonic max generation and writer-side-CAS-rejects stale lease writes (ADR-068)

nodes.technology: Bare git repos over private net

description.txt: Zero-trust inbound access — no exposed ports
Placement, health-reschedule, and rolling deploys across the Hetzner cluster (ADR-068 Phase 4a)

Co-located stateless sticky router (in each web-host process, not a separate box) — resolves a session's owning host from the per-user worktree lease at the WS-upgrade handshake; local ⇒ serve, remote ⇒ proxy over one-way-TLS private net. Control ops are host-local (no cross-host forwarding). Lease in Postgres (ADR-068 user-sticky amendment)

Cluster of Hetzner Cloud hosts (Nomad clients, spread placement) running web app + CLI engine containers; per-user worktrees on host-local NVMe (ADR-068). At first boot a fresh host pulls the web app image from GHCR and extracts its baked bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl via docker cp (ADR-080, #5921)
Durable trigger layer for server-side agents — cron, oneshot, and HTTP-armed reminders (ADR-030)

PRIVATE GHCR registry (ghcr.io/jikig-ai/soleur-web-platform + soleur-inngest-bootstrap) — source of the web app image a Hetzner host pulls (authenticated via a scoped read:packages credential; the host docker login is done by ci-deploy.sh / the fresh-boot bootstrap, #6005). The image also bakes the host bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl, extracted via docker cp at first boot to keep cloud-init user_data under Hetzner's 32,768-byte cap (ADR-080, #5921).
Users, BYOK-encrypted API keys, conversation sessions

nodes.title: Supabase PostgreSQL (Inngest)

description.txt: Inngest config + run history. Dedicated project isolates connection budget + fate from the main app DB (#5450). Public tables RLS-locked; reachable only as the postgres owner (ADR-030 I8)

nodes.technology: PostgreSQL — dedicated EU project, Supavisor session pooler :5432

description.txt: Inngest durable queue + run-state. AOF on the persistent volume survives a host re-provision — the armed-reminder durability fix (#5450)

description.txt: A workspace Owner using Soleur as their AI organization. Workspaces may have MULTIPLE Owners (ADR-038, ADR-073 team workspaces); Owner-shared surfaces (e.g. the operator email-triage inbox, ADR-066) are readable by every Owner, not just one founder.
Conversation UI, knowledge base viewer, session management
Privacy-focused website analytics

REST endpoints for auth, sessions, and agent control. Registers in-process Agent-SDK options.hooks (sandbox, subagent-audit, phase-surface, tool-attempt-telemetry) on web agent sessions — settingSources:[] isolates these from the CLI .claude/ shell Hook Engine (ADR-070 #5772, #5843).
Inbound email ingress (operator-inbox webhook, svix-verified) + transactional outbound (statutory triage notifications). ADR-066.
Conversation UI, knowledge base viewer, session management

REST endpoints for auth, sessions, and agent control. Registers in-process Agent-SDK options.hooks (sandbox, subagent-audit, phase-surface, tool-attempt-telemetry) on web agent sessions — settingSources:[] isolates these from the CLI .claude/ shell Hook Engine (ADR-070 #5772, #5843).
Payment processing and subscription billing
